name: Plantation-Style Catfish Fillets
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw frozen fish according to package directions.
2. Mix flour with salt and pepper; add paprika to taste. Dust the fillets evenly with the seasoned mixture.
3. In a large, heavy skillet, heat shortening over medium-high heat. Add fillets in a single layer and sauté until crisp and golden brown, turning occasionally. Remove fillets with a slotted spoon and set aside on a plate.
4. In the same skillet, add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in tomatoes and cook until heated through, about 2 minutes.
5. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the tomatoes to a bowl. Reserve the sauce in the skillet.
6. Add spinach to the reserved sauce and stir until just tender. Arrange spinach around the tomatoes.
7. Place the fillets on top of the spinach mixture.
8. Combine the remaining Parmesan cheese with the lemon juice, pepper, and sour cream. Spoon this mixture over the fillets.
9. Sprinkle any remaining cheese on top. Under a broiler set to high heat, brown the cheese until melted. Serve immediately.
